Alliance microprocesses in early therapy: Predicting goal consensus in face-to-face and videoconferencing psychotherapy

Supplementary Figures - Model-predicted probabilities of goal consensus by behavior/sequence and modality

A Bayesian logistic regression model was fitted using the brms package (Bürkner, 2017) to predict goal consensus strength (strong vs. weak) from the presence of a behavior or a sequence of behaviors and treatment modality (face-to-face [F2F] vs. videoconferencing psychotherapy [VCP]), and their interaction. Estimated marginal means, contrasts, and model-implied predicted probabilities were computed using emmeans (Lenth, 2024), yielding posterior median odds ratios and 95% highest posterior density (HPD) intervals on the response scale. The figures display posterior predicted probabilities of strong versus weak goal consensus in the probability scale (0-1), as a function of behavior presence, separately for each modality; points represent predicted probabilities, lines connect behavior conditions (absence vs. presence) within alliance groups, and facets distinguish F2F and VCP. The interpretation of each figure can be accessed by clicking on the plot.
